item.title: Steam Machine Reservations Are Being Scalped for Insane Prices
item.description:
Steam Machines are appearing on reseller sites and successfully selling for insane mark-ups, with some of the units going for double the standard price or more. While scalping isn't unusual, none of these sellers actually have a Steam Machine in hand, as they haven't gone up for sale yet from Valve.

Steam Machines will likely be in short supply, which is probably helping to propel these listings and sales. Valve noted that it has only been able to produce about two-thirds of the quantity it wanted to make available, due to parts shortages plaguing the industry right now. Given that Steam Decks were frequently out of stock following their launch before the AI hardware crisis was on the horizon, it's likely that the availability of Steam Machines will be even worse.

Steam Machines Are Being Scalped Before They're Even Secured

Steam Machine listings have been appearing on eBay, with many of the listings being priced at $1,000 or more over the cost of buying one directly from Valve. While there are many still listed, several have already sold. For example, one listing of the cheapest Steam Machine sold for $2,000, which would normally sell for $1,049 direct from Valve. A pair of 2TB Steam Machines sold for $2,700 and $2,900 respectively, with it normally costing $1,349 solo or $1,428 with a controller. Perhaps the most mind-boggling price belongs to a 512GB Steam Machine with a controller that sold on eBay for $2,800, which would normally cost $1,128.

Choose cover art to complete the equations.

10.0 s
0 / 10

Results

0

High Score: 0

—

More Games
Choose cover art to complete the equations.
Easy (15s)Medium (10s)Hard (5s)
10.0 s
0 / 10
Results
0
High Score: 0 —
More Games

The most shocking part of the sales, however, is that none of the sellers have made a purchase yet, as sales have not officially opened with Valve. Instead, these sellers only have emails confirming they've been selected to buy one once the consoles are actually put on sale. Obviously, some fans of Valve are willing to spend exorbitant amounts in order to secure the console, even though many have balked at even the standard prices for the systems.

Valve has shared why the Steam Machine is as expensive as it is, explaining that it isn't subsidizing the Steam Machine the way that major console manufacturers sometimes do. Interestingly, it seems that this is a change from the way the Steam Deck was handled, as Valve CEO Gabe Newell noted that the Deck's pricing was "very aggressive" and "painful." However, it had to compete with the Nintendo Switch, which is one of the most popular game consoles of all time.

Test your knowledge.

Sign in to claim your place on the leaderboard!

Leaderboard
No scores yet. Be the first.
1/10

Question text goes here?

Results

0 Points

—

Sign in to claim your place on the leaderboard!

Leaderboard
No scores yet. Be the first.

For the moment, it remains to be seen how the future will play out for the Steam Machine and those who are hoping to buy one. Some have started to be wary about the system, particularly after Valve quietly changed details regarding the Steam Machine's 4K and FPS capabilities. However, if the system lives up to the hype, even more gamers may end up wanting to buy one. With the AI boom leading to severe parts shortages all over the world, though, Valve could potentially struggle to meet demand for years to come. It's ultimately up to gamers who didn't make it into the purchase queue to decide whether they want to try and wait or spend way more than MSRP to secure one.
item.content:encoded:
Steam Machines are appearing on reseller sites and successfully selling for insane mark-ups, with some of the units going for double the standard price or more. While scalping isn't unusual, none of these sellers actually have a Steam Machine in hand, as they haven't gone up for sale yet from Valve.

Steam Machines will likely be in short supply, which is probably helping to propel these listings and sales. Valve noted that it has only been able to produce about two-thirds of the quantity it wanted to make available, due to parts shortages plaguing the industry right now. Given that Steam Decks were frequently out of stock following their launch before the AI hardware crisis was on the horizon, it's likely that the availability of Steam Machines will be even worse.

Steam Machines Are Being Scalped Before They're Even Secured

Steam Machine listings have been appearing on eBay, with many of the listings being priced at $1,000 or more over the cost of buying one directly from Valve. While there are many still listed, several have already sold. For example, one listing of the cheapest Steam Machine sold for $2,000, which would normally sell for $1,049 direct from Valve. A pair of 2TB Steam Machines sold for $2,700 and $2,900 respectively, with it normally costing $1,349 solo or $1,428 with a controller. Perhaps the most mind-boggling price belongs to a 512GB Steam Machine with a controller that sold on eBay for $2,800, which would normally cost $1,128.

Choose cover art to complete the equations.

10.0 s
0 / 10

Results

0

High Score: 0

—

More Games
Choose cover art to complete the equations.
Easy (15s)Medium (10s)Hard (5s)
10.0 s
0 / 10
Results
0
High Score: 0 —
More Games

The most shocking part of the sales, however, is that none of the sellers have made a purchase yet, as sales have not officially opened with Valve. Instead, these sellers only have emails confirming they've been selected to buy one once the consoles are actually put on sale. Obviously, some fans of Valve are willing to spend exorbitant amounts in order to secure the console, even though many have balked at even the standard prices for the systems.

Valve has shared why the Steam Machine is as expensive as it is, explaining that it isn't subsidizing the Steam Machine the way that major console manufacturers sometimes do. Interestingly, it seems that this is a change from the way the Steam Deck was handled, as Valve CEO Gabe Newell noted that the Deck's pricing was "very aggressive" and "painful." However, it had to compete with the Nintendo Switch, which is one of the most popular game consoles of all time.

Test your knowledge.

Sign in to claim your place on the leaderboard!

Leaderboard
No scores yet. Be the first.
1/10

Question text goes here?

Results

0 Points

—

Sign in to claim your place on the leaderboard!

Leaderboard
No scores yet. Be the first.

For the moment, it remains to be seen how the future will play out for the Steam Machine and those who are hoping to buy one. Some have started to be wary about the system, particularly after Valve quietly changed details regarding the Steam Machine's 4K and FPS capabilities. However, if the system lives up to the hype, even more gamers may end up wanting to buy one. With the AI boom leading to severe parts shortages all over the world, though, Valve could potentially struggle to meet demand for years to come. It's ultimately up to gamers who didn't make it into the purchase queue to decide whether they want to try and wait or spend way more than MSRP to secure one.

item.title: Clash Royale Balance Changes for July 2026 (Season 85)
item.description:
Clash Royale gets a rebalance change every month to shake up the meta. For the 85th season, several popular cards are getting adjusted. Season 85 brings balance changes to six cards , and Heroes make up most of the list. If you are wondering which cards got buffed and which ones got nerfed, here's everything you need to know about the Clash Royale July 2026 balance changes.

​

Clash Royale Balance Changes for July 2026 (Season 85)

Supercell has revealed the first draft of the Season 85 balance changes, and six cards are currently on the list. This time around, four cards are getting nerfed, one is receiving a buff, and Hero Tombstone is getting a rework . Here's what's changing:

  • Shield HP Nerf for Hero Knight
  • Teleport and Ability Shot Nerf for Hero Magic Archer
  • Landing Damage Nerf for Hero Balloon
  • Close Quarters Damage Nerf for Evo Executioner
  • HP Buff for Ram Rider
  • Rework for Hero Tombstone.

​

Keep in mind that these changes are still a work in progress, so some of the numbers or even the changes themselves could be adjusted before the update goes live.

​

All Nerfs in Clash Royale’s Balance Changes for July

Hero Knight Nerfs (& Our Thoughts)

Hero Knight is getting a nerf to its shield. Right now, activating its ability gives it a shield with 768 HP on top of its health. After the update, that shield will have 512 HP instead. That's a 33% reduction.

I honestly think this nerf was overdue. Hero Knight has been the best Hero in the game for a while now, and his ability gets value in almost every match. Even after losing a third of its shield HP, I don't think this is going to push it out of the meta.

Hero Magic Archer Nerfs (& Our Thoughts)

When you activate Hero Magic Archer's first ability, it currently teleports back five tiles. After the update, that distance will be reduced to four tiles . Its first attack after teleporting, the Triple Threat Shot, is also losing some range. It currently reaches 15.4 tiles, but that will reduce to 14 tiles . Overall, that's a 20% nerf to its teleport distance, and a 9% nerf to the range of its Triple Threat Shot.

I don't think these nerfs will kill the card, but I can see a lot of other players switching her out of their decks, since Hero Magic Archer was annoying to come up against in the right deck.

Hero Balloon Nerfs (& Our Thoughts)

Hero Balloon's ability summons a Skeletrooper that deals landing damage before attacking nearby troops. After the update, that landing damage will drop from 256 to 204, which is a 20% nerf.

I think this nerf is going to hurt more than it looks. One of the best things about Hero Balloon was how easily the Skeletrooper eliminated support troops after landing. With less damage, it could miss out on some important interactions against low HP support troops like Dart Goblin and Archers. We will have to see how those interactions play out once the update goes live.

Hero Balloon already took a landing damage nerf last month, so seeing another nerf this soon is a bit surprising. Even so, I don't think this is enough to knock it out of the meta.

Evo Executioner Nerfs (& Our Thoughts)

Evo Executioner is the only Evolution getting changed in the July balance changes. Supercell is reducing the range of its Close Quarters effect by 14%. Before the update, enemy troops within 3.5 tiles would take 60% extra damage. After the update, that range will be reduced to 3 tiles. This means you will have to get a little closer to get the bonus damage.

This nerf was probably only a matter of time. Evo Executioner has been one of the strongest defensive evolutions in Clash Royale . The shorter Close Quarters range will definitely make it less threatening, but I don't think this changes much overall. It still has the knockback effect, which is the biggest reason people use it in the first place. And it still takes only one cycle to evolve.

All Buffs in Clash Royale’s Balance Changes for July

Ram Rider Buffs(& Our Thoughts)

Ram Rider is the only card getting a buff in the July balance changes. Its HP is increasing by 4%, going from 1,697 to 1,766 . It might not sound like a huge change, but those extra hit points should help it survive a little longer and, in some matches, get an extra hit on the tower.

I think Ram Rider needed this buff. It hasn't been a popular win condition for quite a while because it can be countered easily. The Hog Rider has simply been the more reliable choice in most situations. I don't think this update changes that overnight, but it does narrow the gap a little.

Hero Tombstone's Rework (& Our Thoughts)

Hero Tombstone is getting a full rework. When it was released, it was meant to be both a defensive building and an offensive win condition. Once you activate its ability, a Skeleton Queen rises from the Tombstone and marches toward the enemy tower. The biggest issue was the six elixir ability cost. It was just too expensive.

Supercell is now reducing the ability cost from six elixir to five . The Skeleton Queen is also getting a big HP buff, going from 3,379 to 4,044 HP, which is a 20% increase . To balance things out, the Skeleton Queen's damage is being reduced by 12%, reducing from 320 to 281 . It will also no longer spawn Skeletons.

I think this is a step in the right direction. The six elixir ability was the biggest reason most players didn’t use the Hero Tombstone. Lowering the elixir cost to five should make it much easier to use.

Losing the Skeleton spawns definitely hurts, but the extra HP should help the Skeleton Queen stay alive long enough to make an impact. I'm looking forward to trying it because I can see this rework giving Hero Tombstone a place in the meta.

Clash Royale

Follow
Followed
Real-Time Strategy
Systems
Released
March 2, 2016
Developer(s)
Supercell
Publisher(s)
Supercell
Engine
Unity
Multiplayer
Online Multiplayer

WHERE TO PLAY

DIGITAL

Genre(s)
Real-Time Strategy
Powered by
item.content:encoded:
Clash Royale gets a rebalance change every month to shake up the meta. For the 85th season, several popular cards are getting adjusted. Season 85 brings balance changes to six cards , and Heroes make up most of the list. If you are wondering which cards got buffed and which ones got nerfed, here's everything you need to know about the Clash Royale July 2026 balance changes.

​

Clash Royale Balance Changes for July 2026 (Season 85)

Supercell has revealed the first draft of the Season 85 balance changes, and six cards are currently on the list. This time around, four cards are getting nerfed, one is receiving a buff, and Hero Tombstone is getting a rework . Here's what's changing:

  • Shield HP Nerf for Hero Knight
  • Teleport and Ability Shot Nerf for Hero Magic Archer
  • Landing Damage Nerf for Hero Balloon
  • Close Quarters Damage Nerf for Evo Executioner
  • HP Buff for Ram Rider
  • Rework for Hero Tombstone.

​

Keep in mind that these changes are still a work in progress, so some of the numbers or even the changes themselves could be adjusted before the update goes live.

​

All Nerfs in Clash Royale’s Balance Changes for July

Hero Knight Nerfs (& Our Thoughts)

Hero Knight is getting a nerf to its shield. Right now, activating its ability gives it a shield with 768 HP on top of its health. After the update, that shield will have 512 HP instead. That's a 33% reduction.

I honestly think this nerf was overdue. Hero Knight has been the best Hero in the game for a while now, and his ability gets value in almost every match. Even after losing a third of its shield HP, I don't think this is going to push it out of the meta.

Hero Magic Archer Nerfs (& Our Thoughts)

When you activate Hero Magic Archer's first ability, it currently teleports back five tiles. After the update, that distance will be reduced to four tiles . Its first attack after teleporting, the Triple Threat Shot, is also losing some range. It currently reaches 15.4 tiles, but that will reduce to 14 tiles . Overall, that's a 20% nerf to its teleport distance, and a 9% nerf to the range of its Triple Threat Shot.

I don't think these nerfs will kill the card, but I can see a lot of other players switching her out of their decks, since Hero Magic Archer was annoying to come up against in the right deck.

Hero Balloon Nerfs (& Our Thoughts)

Hero Balloon's ability summons a Skeletrooper that deals landing damage before attacking nearby troops. After the update, that landing damage will drop from 256 to 204, which is a 20% nerf.

I think this nerf is going to hurt more than it looks. One of the best things about Hero Balloon was how easily the Skeletrooper eliminated support troops after landing. With less damage, it could miss out on some important interactions against low HP support troops like Dart Goblin and Archers. We will have to see how those interactions play out once the update goes live.

Hero Balloon already took a landing damage nerf last month, so seeing another nerf this soon is a bit surprising. Even so, I don't think this is enough to knock it out of the meta.

Evo Executioner Nerfs (& Our Thoughts)

Evo Executioner is the only Evolution getting changed in the July balance changes. Supercell is reducing the range of its Close Quarters effect by 14%. Before the update, enemy troops within 3.5 tiles would take 60% extra damage. After the update, that range will be reduced to 3 tiles. This means you will have to get a little closer to get the bonus damage.

This nerf was probably only a matter of time. Evo Executioner has been one of the strongest defensive evolutions in Clash Royale . The shorter Close Quarters range will definitely make it less threatening, but I don't think this changes much overall. It still has the knockback effect, which is the biggest reason people use it in the first place. And it still takes only one cycle to evolve.

All Buffs in Clash Royale’s Balance Changes for July

Ram Rider Buffs(& Our Thoughts)

Ram Rider is the only card getting a buff in the July balance changes. Its HP is increasing by 4%, going from 1,697 to 1,766 . It might not sound like a huge change, but those extra hit points should help it survive a little longer and, in some matches, get an extra hit on the tower.

I think Ram Rider needed this buff. It hasn't been a popular win condition for quite a while because it can be countered easily. The Hog Rider has simply been the more reliable choice in most situations. I don't think this update changes that overnight, but it does narrow the gap a little.

Hero Tombstone's Rework (& Our Thoughts)

Hero Tombstone is getting a full rework. When it was released, it was meant to be both a defensive building and an offensive win condition. Once you activate its ability, a Skeleton Queen rises from the Tombstone and marches toward the enemy tower. The biggest issue was the six elixir ability cost. It was just too expensive.

Supercell is now reducing the ability cost from six elixir to five . The Skeleton Queen is also getting a big HP buff, going from 3,379 to 4,044 HP, which is a 20% increase . To balance things out, the Skeleton Queen's damage is being reduced by 12%, reducing from 320 to 281 . It will also no longer spawn Skeletons.

I think this is a step in the right direction. The six elixir ability was the biggest reason most players didn’t use the Hero Tombstone. Lowering the elixir cost to five should make it much easier to use.

Losing the Skeleton spawns definitely hurts, but the extra HP should help the Skeleton Queen stay alive long enough to make an impact. I'm looking forward to trying it because I can see this rework giving Hero Tombstone a place in the meta.

Clash Royale

Follow
Followed
Real-Time Strategy
Systems
Released
March 2, 2016
Developer(s)
Supercell
Publisher(s)
Supercell
Engine
Unity
Multiplayer
Online Multiplayer

WHERE TO PLAY

DIGITAL

Genre(s)
Real-Time Strategy
Powered by

item.title: Where to Find All Pink Coins (MysteryKey) in Deltarune Chapter 5
item.description:
Every chapter in Deltarune features a secret boss, and Chapter 5 is no different. This time, you'll come across a MysteryKey that is presumably needed to access the encounter, but purchasing it requires a new currency called Pink Coins .

Thankfully, there are 19 Pink Coins scattered throughout the chapter, and collecting them all is worthwhile since they have uses beyond just the MysteryKey. The guide below covers every Pink Coin location and where to use the MysteryKey afterward.

All Pink Coin Locations in Deltarune Chapter 5

#1

From the Garden area, where you obtain your first Watering Can, water the path on the left to reveal a route leading into a room filled with water. Head all the way up to find a chest containing the first Pink Coin.

#2

After acquiring the Petal Feather at the shrine, the gameplay changes into a 2D platformer. Jump onto the lamp post on the left, then onto the leaf platforms. Reach the top platform and jump to the right to land on top of the shrine. Continue into the next room, where you'll find the Pink Coin hanging by a thread on the right.

#3

After climbing down the vine next to the Exit marker, head right to the main path. Following the cutscene, activate the Power Structure on the right. Instead of climbing the vines, continue all the way left. At the end, jump up to reveal and collect the third Pink Coin.

#4

Head to the room where you first meet the jumping flower beside the giant loaf of bread. If you've unlocked Fast Travel, simply warp to Hopschef. Activate the Power Structure on the right and continue right to fight three enemies. After defeating them, use the next Power Structure to return to normal gameplay and enter the doorway.

Go through the Torii Gate while dodging the incoming projectiles from the flying enemy. At the other end, ring the bell to drop the chest and collect the Pink Coin.

#5

In the Cliffs area, continue until you reach the first room with a Power Structure. Climb all the way up until you find another one. Ignore it for now. Instead, use Ralsei's ACT on the vine to the left and intentionally fall so he follows you. Stand on Ralsei and immediately jump into the small space on the left to grab the Pink Coin.

#6

From the previous collectible, activate the Power Structure and ride the wind upward. In the next area, head left and climb the vines. Move across to the platform on the right and interact with the fox to reveal more vines. Climb up, continue right until you see water falling onto the Pink Coin, then use the waterfall to safely drop onto it.

#7

After passing the shop and the wheat field, you'll reach another Power Structure. Activate it and use Ralsei's ACT to move upward. Ignore the nearby Power Structure and instead use Susie's ACT on the fan to redirect the wind to the right.

Drop onto the moving leaf platform, then fall to the left. Use Ralsei's ACT on the lower moving platforms to reach the room on the right. Bounce on the umbrella, defeat the enemies, then continue right to collect the hanging Pink Coin.

#8

In the area with the fake chests, continue into the next room and run through the Torii Gate. During the story sequence, you'll be forced upward, so return here afterward. Continue further right and jump across the gap to find the Pink Coin.

#9

Inside the Castle, head right from the Foyer into the Café and continue into the next room. Interact with the pot statue near the edge so it falls. Enter the next area filled with pot statues and defeat the enemy pot.

Knock down every pot statue, then return to the Castle entrance. Go through the door to the right of the Light World exit. Push the pots onto the pressure plates to open the right path and collect the Pink Coin.

#10

From the previous collectible, head up the path. You'll notice the shadow of a chest on your left. Continue through the middle path before heading left to open it and collect the Pink Coin.

#11

In the room beside the Orange Door featuring the invisible platform puzzle, interact with the table and arrange the shapes so that:

  • The horizontal rectangle sits below the first red line.
  • The vertical rectangle touches the right side while connecting both the upper and lower screens.

Afterward, activate the Power Source and make your way to the top right corner of the room to collect the Pink Coin.

#12

In the room with the rotating orange boxing gloves, you'll notice a white wall on the right. Hit the gloves so they smash through the wall, then enter the hidden room and strike the orange glove again to obtain the Pink Coin.

#13

Inside the room where you search for the three foxes for the fake Aqua, locate all five foxes. Doing so unlocks the door on the left, where you'll find the Pink Coin. If you're missing any, check out our All Foxes guide.

#14

Head up from the Fox Puzzle room to find another Power Source. Activate it and head left into the optional invisible platform puzzle instead of taking the main route. Arrange the puzzle as follows:

  • Move the smaller square to the right so its top and bottom corners touch both screens while remaining centered.
  • Position the other square so it touches the upper left corner of the smaller square.

If needed, refer to the images for the exact solution. Once complete, head up and hit the hanging bell to receive the Pink Coin.

#15

Continue to the next save point outside the Orange Door. Head down the path on the right. Interact with the first mushroom and place it on the left to remove the spikes. For the second mushroom, carry it from the far right and hurry back before the fox eats it. Once the path opens, grab the Pink Coin from the chest.

#16

Go through the second Yellow Door and activate the Power Source. Instead of heading left toward the main path, go right into the room with the flower bounce objects. Bounce your way upward to reach the Pink Coin.

#17

Go through the Blue Door, and once you're back outside, avoid entering the next Blue Door that starts the Blue and Yellow boss fight. Instead, head down and left.

Activate the Power Source and carefully make your way across without getting hit. Hit the bell on the left to obtain the Pink Coin.

#18

After Green's final shop, head right and climb to the top. When the main path continues to the right, go left instead. Use the bounce objects to travel all the way to the far left, where you'll find another Pink Coin.

#19

From the previous collectible, return to the middle of the room and drop down. Activate the fan to descend past several spiked flowers and guns. At the bottom, you'll find the Pink Door along with the final Pink Coin.

With all 19 Pink Coins collected, return to the shop in the Cliffs and purchase the MysteryKey for 10 Pink Coins. Save your remaining coins for the powerful weapons and armor that become available after defeating the secret boss behind the Pink Door.

More Deltarune Guides

If you need help with some of the Deltarune guides, make sure to check these out:

  • How to Get Chapter 5's Secret Egg
  • What To Do With Flowery Dollars
  • All Cafe Break Interactions in Chapter 5
  • Everything to Do at the Festival

Deltarune

Follow
Followed
RPG
Systems
OpenCritic Reviews
Top Critic Avg: 86 /100 Critics Rec: 83%
Released
October 31, 2018
ESRB
Teen / Language, Suggestive Themes, Mild Blood, Fantasy Violence
Developer(s)
Toby Fox
Publisher(s)
Toby Fox
10 Images

WHERE TO PLAY

DIGITAL
PHYSICAL

Genre(s)
RPG
Powered by
item.content:encoded:
Every chapter in Deltarune features a secret boss, and Chapter 5 is no different. This time, you'll come across a MysteryKey that is presumably needed to access the encounter, but purchasing it requires a new currency called Pink Coins .

Thankfully, there are 19 Pink Coins scattered throughout the chapter, and collecting them all is worthwhile since they have uses beyond just the MysteryKey. The guide below covers every Pink Coin location and where to use the MysteryKey afterward.

All Pink Coin Locations in Deltarune Chapter 5

#1

From the Garden area, where you obtain your first Watering Can, water the path on the left to reveal a route leading into a room filled with water. Head all the way up to find a chest containing the first Pink Coin.

#2

After acquiring the Petal Feather at the shrine, the gameplay changes into a 2D platformer. Jump onto the lamp post on the left, then onto the leaf platforms. Reach the top platform and jump to the right to land on top of the shrine. Continue into the next room, where you'll find the Pink Coin hanging by a thread on the right.

#3

After climbing down the vine next to the Exit marker, head right to the main path. Following the cutscene, activate the Power Structure on the right. Instead of climbing the vines, continue all the way left. At the end, jump up to reveal and collect the third Pink Coin.

#4

Head to the room where you first meet the jumping flower beside the giant loaf of bread. If you've unlocked Fast Travel, simply warp to Hopschef. Activate the Power Structure on the right and continue right to fight three enemies. After defeating them, use the next Power Structure to return to normal gameplay and enter the doorway.

Go through the Torii Gate while dodging the incoming projectiles from the flying enemy. At the other end, ring the bell to drop the chest and collect the Pink Coin.

#5

In the Cliffs area, continue until you reach the first room with a Power Structure. Climb all the way up until you find another one. Ignore it for now. Instead, use Ralsei's ACT on the vine to the left and intentionally fall so he follows you. Stand on Ralsei and immediately jump into the small space on the left to grab the Pink Coin.

#6

From the previous collectible, activate the Power Structure and ride the wind upward. In the next area, head left and climb the vines. Move across to the platform on the right and interact with the fox to reveal more vines. Climb up, continue right until you see water falling onto the Pink Coin, then use the waterfall to safely drop onto it.

#7

After passing the shop and the wheat field, you'll reach another Power Structure. Activate it and use Ralsei's ACT to move upward. Ignore the nearby Power Structure and instead use Susie's ACT on the fan to redirect the wind to the right.

Drop onto the moving leaf platform, then fall to the left. Use Ralsei's ACT on the lower moving platforms to reach the room on the right. Bounce on the umbrella, defeat the enemies, then continue right to collect the hanging Pink Coin.

#8

In the area with the fake chests, continue into the next room and run through the Torii Gate. During the story sequence, you'll be forced upward, so return here afterward. Continue further right and jump across the gap to find the Pink Coin.

#9

Inside the Castle, head right from the Foyer into the Café and continue into the next room. Interact with the pot statue near the edge so it falls. Enter the next area filled with pot statues and defeat the enemy pot.

Knock down every pot statue, then return to the Castle entrance. Go through the door to the right of the Light World exit. Push the pots onto the pressure plates to open the right path and collect the Pink Coin.

#10

From the previous collectible, head up the path. You'll notice the shadow of a chest on your left. Continue through the middle path before heading left to open it and collect the Pink Coin.

#11

In the room beside the Orange Door featuring the invisible platform puzzle, interact with the table and arrange the shapes so that:

  • The horizontal rectangle sits below the first red line.
  • The vertical rectangle touches the right side while connecting both the upper and lower screens.

Afterward, activate the Power Source and make your way to the top right corner of the room to collect the Pink Coin.

#12

In the room with the rotating orange boxing gloves, you'll notice a white wall on the right. Hit the gloves so they smash through the wall, then enter the hidden room and strike the orange glove again to obtain the Pink Coin.

#13

Inside the room where you search for the three foxes for the fake Aqua, locate all five foxes. Doing so unlocks the door on the left, where you'll find the Pink Coin. If you're missing any, check out our All Foxes guide.

#14

Head up from the Fox Puzzle room to find another Power Source. Activate it and head left into the optional invisible platform puzzle instead of taking the main route. Arrange the puzzle as follows:

  • Move the smaller square to the right so its top and bottom corners touch both screens while remaining centered.
  • Position the other square so it touches the upper left corner of the smaller square.

If needed, refer to the images for the exact solution. Once complete, head up and hit the hanging bell to receive the Pink Coin.

#15

Continue to the next save point outside the Orange Door. Head down the path on the right. Interact with the first mushroom and place it on the left to remove the spikes. For the second mushroom, carry it from the far right and hurry back before the fox eats it. Once the path opens, grab the Pink Coin from the chest.

#16

Go through the second Yellow Door and activate the Power Source. Instead of heading left toward the main path, go right into the room with the flower bounce objects. Bounce your way upward to reach the Pink Coin.

#17

Go through the Blue Door, and once you're back outside, avoid entering the next Blue Door that starts the Blue and Yellow boss fight. Instead, head down and left.

Activate the Power Source and carefully make your way across without getting hit. Hit the bell on the left to obtain the Pink Coin.

#18

After Green's final shop, head right and climb to the top. When the main path continues to the right, go left instead. Use the bounce objects to travel all the way to the far left, where you'll find another Pink Coin.

#19

From the previous collectible, return to the middle of the room and drop down. Activate the fan to descend past several spiked flowers and guns. At the bottom, you'll find the Pink Door along with the final Pink Coin.

With all 19 Pink Coins collected, return to the shop in the Cliffs and purchase the MysteryKey for 10 Pink Coins. Save your remaining coins for the powerful weapons and armor that become available after defeating the secret boss behind the Pink Door.

More Deltarune Guides

If you need help with some of the Deltarune guides, make sure to check these out:

  • How to Get Chapter 5's Secret Egg
  • What To Do With Flowery Dollars
  • All Cafe Break Interactions in Chapter 5
  • Everything to Do at the Festival

Deltarune

Follow
Followed
RPG
Systems
OpenCritic Reviews
Top Critic Avg: 86 /100 Critics Rec: 83%
Released
October 31, 2018
ESRB
Teen / Language, Suggestive Themes, Mild Blood, Fantasy Violence
Developer(s)
Toby Fox
Publisher(s)
Toby Fox
10 Images

WHERE TO PLAY

DIGITAL
PHYSICAL

Genre(s)
RPG
Powered by

item.title: Star Fox Review
item.description:
In 1993, Nintendo released Star Fox for the SNES, a highly innovative rail shooter that told the story of anthropomorphic animals battling it out in spaceships. In 1997, Nintendo retold and reimagined Star Fox 's story in Star Fox 64 . In 2011, Nintendo remade Star Fox 64 for the 3DS as Star Fox 64 3D . The Star Fox story was retold yet again in Star Fox Zero on the Wii U in 2016. And now, in 2026, Nintendo has released yet another Star Fox that effectively tells the same story, but this time on the Switch 2.

Star Fox on the Switch 2 is a remake of the Nintendo 64 game, with near-identical level design and core gameplay, but with significantly improved graphics. Like the N64 game before it, Star Fox 's campaign sees players bounce between different planets of the Lylat System in pursuit of the villainous Dr. Andross, completing a nice variety of missions objectives along the way using the titular Star Fox team's Arwing ships.

The Classic Star Fox Campaign Returns (Again)

A short-but-sweet experience, every level in Star Fox offers something different. One level will have players battling enemy forces in outer space dogfights, while another has them withstanding the extreme temperatures of a lava planet, complete with a boss fight against a giant magma monster. Most missions have Fox and crew piloting their Arwings, but there's also a submarine and a tank level on occasion to help spice things up.

Star Fox is a game that's meant to be replayed over and over again, with alternate routes and secrets to discover. Fulfilling the conditions necessary to find Star Fox 's alternate routes and unlock new levels is a thrill, and makes it so replaying the campaign multiple times never gets old. It helps that the core gameplay is plenty action-packed and a lot of fun, of course.

Star Fox on the Switch 2 doesn't differ much from the Nintendo 64 game in terms of gameplay. In fact, they're virtually identical. Players have standard lasers and powerful bombs at their disposal to destroy any enemies unlucky enough to get in their path, and the vehicles have tricks that make things a little more engaging than many other rail shooters. For example, the Arwing can perform barrel rolls to deflect enemy attacks, and the tank can briefly hover to get past obstacles and collect otherwise out-of-reach power-ups.

Something new the Switch 2 version does introduce is mouse controls, which work very well and offer a fresh way to experience classic Star Fox action. I think most fans will want to stick with a more traditional control scheme, but Star Fox offers perhaps the best use of Switch 2 mouse controls to date. They don't take long to adjust to, and they help what is nearly a 30-year-old gaming experience feel at least a little fresh.

The fresh coat of paint also goes a long way in helping Star Fox on Switch 2 stand out from its predecessors. Nintendo opted to go for a more photo-realistic take on Star Fox 's characters and worlds, with vastly improved graphics that make it unrecognizable compared to the older games. The new Star Fox character designs aren't for everybody, but they are undeniably detailed, and everything looks sharp, with top-notch performance to boot. New and expanded cutscenes are also in Star Fox on Switch 2, though I was disappointed that the much-touted prologue mission starring Fox's dad, James McCloud, is merely a cutscene and not playable.

Just like the graphics are miles ahead of the older games, the voice acting is as well (even if it's not exactly perfect), and while some of the campy charm is lost, it's an improvement overall. Out of all the times Nintendo has retold the Star Fox story, the Switch 2 version is easily the best-looking and best-acted. Star Fox 's campaign on Switch 2 is an improvement over the previous iterations of it thanks to these upgrades, but those that have already played the older versions extensively might find it hard to justify spending $59.99 on what is, at its core, an experience already available on Nintendo Switch 2 through the Switch Online Expansion Pack's N64 games library.

Star Fox Multiplayer is a Mixed Bag

Others will likely take issue with Star Fox 's co-op implementation. Instead of doing a proper co-op mode for the campaign, Star Fox on Switch 2 opts for one of Nintendo's cop-out co-op modes, which is simply a far inferior way to play the game. In Star Fox 's case, Nintendo basically splits functionality between the two players, so one person controls the Arwing's movements and the other controls the guns. It's not an ideal way to experience Star Fox , and it's hard to shake the feeling that the mode was tacked on for the sake of saying that the game has a co-op campaign. It's not true co-op, and if you go into this expecting true co-op, you are going to be greatly disappointed.

But when it comes to the biggest disappointment in the Star Fox remake on Switch 2, it's the complete lack of split-screen multiplayer. Star Fox 64 split-screen was a blast back in the day, but that's been removed with the Switch 2 version. Star Fox supports GameShare so it is at least a little easier to still have local multiplayer matches, but the lack of split-screen is the game's biggest misstep by far.

If you can get past the lack of split-screen, Star Fox's PvP multiplayer is fun, but light on content. Star Fox multiplayer battles are 4v4 fights where players can compete against local friends on other Switch consoles, bots, or online players. Star Fox only has three multiplayer maps at launch, and so it gets repetitive pretty quick, but multiplayer-specific power-ups and the competitive thrill keep it at least somewhat engaging.

"Beating" Star Fox on Nintendo Switch 2 takes 1.5 - 2 hours depending on skill level, but there are alternate routes, a Challenge Mode to tackle, and multiplayer to take into account.

While the multiplayer is fun, for the most part, if you have played Star Fox on Nintendo 64 or 3DS, you have effectively already played Star Fox on Nintendo Switch 2. That makes the $59.99 price tag hard to swallow, but if you are someone who has never played Star Fox before, you will likely get a lot more mileage out of the Switch 2 remake.

6 /10

Star Fox

Follow
Followed
Rail Shooter
Systems
Released
June 25, 2026
ESRB
Everyone 10+ / Fantasy Violence
Developer(s)
Nintendo
Publisher(s)
Nintendo
Multiplayer
Online Co-Op
5 Images

WHERE TO PLAY

DIGITAL

Genre(s)
Rail Shooter
Pros & Cons
  • Gorgeous graphics and top-notch performance make this the best way to experience Star Fox
  • Solid PvP experience
  • It's just Star Fox again
  • No split-screen multiplayer
  • Lackluster co-op mode
  • Multiplayer is light on content

Star Fox is available now, exclusively for the Nintendo Switch 2.
item.content:encoded:
In 1993, Nintendo released Star Fox for the SNES, a highly innovative rail shooter that told the story of anthropomorphic animals battling it out in spaceships. In 1997, Nintendo retold and reimagined Star Fox 's story in Star Fox 64 . In 2011, Nintendo remade Star Fox 64 for the 3DS as Star Fox 64 3D . The Star Fox story was retold yet again in Star Fox Zero on the Wii U in 2016. And now, in 2026, Nintendo has released yet another Star Fox that effectively tells the same story, but this time on the Switch 2.

Star Fox on the Switch 2 is a remake of the Nintendo 64 game, with near-identical level design and core gameplay, but with significantly improved graphics. Like the N64 game before it, Star Fox 's campaign sees players bounce between different planets of the Lylat System in pursuit of the villainous Dr. Andross, completing a nice variety of missions objectives along the way using the titular Star Fox team's Arwing ships.

The Classic Star Fox Campaign Returns (Again)

A short-but-sweet experience, every level in Star Fox offers something different. One level will have players battling enemy forces in outer space dogfights, while another has them withstanding the extreme temperatures of a lava planet, complete with a boss fight against a giant magma monster. Most missions have Fox and crew piloting their Arwings, but there's also a submarine and a tank level on occasion to help spice things up.

Star Fox is a game that's meant to be replayed over and over again, with alternate routes and secrets to discover. Fulfilling the conditions necessary to find Star Fox 's alternate routes and unlock new levels is a thrill, and makes it so replaying the campaign multiple times never gets old. It helps that the core gameplay is plenty action-packed and a lot of fun, of course.

Star Fox on the Switch 2 doesn't differ much from the Nintendo 64 game in terms of gameplay. In fact, they're virtually identical. Players have standard lasers and powerful bombs at their disposal to destroy any enemies unlucky enough to get in their path, and the vehicles have tricks that make things a little more engaging than many other rail shooters. For example, the Arwing can perform barrel rolls to deflect enemy attacks, and the tank can briefly hover to get past obstacles and collect otherwise out-of-reach power-ups.

Something new the Switch 2 version does introduce is mouse controls, which work very well and offer a fresh way to experience classic Star Fox action. I think most fans will want to stick with a more traditional control scheme, but Star Fox offers perhaps the best use of Switch 2 mouse controls to date. They don't take long to adjust to, and they help what is nearly a 30-year-old gaming experience feel at least a little fresh.

The fresh coat of paint also goes a long way in helping Star Fox on Switch 2 stand out from its predecessors. Nintendo opted to go for a more photo-realistic take on Star Fox 's characters and worlds, with vastly improved graphics that make it unrecognizable compared to the older games. The new Star Fox character designs aren't for everybody, but they are undeniably detailed, and everything looks sharp, with top-notch performance to boot. New and expanded cutscenes are also in Star Fox on Switch 2, though I was disappointed that the much-touted prologue mission starring Fox's dad, James McCloud, is merely a cutscene and not playable.

Just like the graphics are miles ahead of the older games, the voice acting is as well (even if it's not exactly perfect), and while some of the campy charm is lost, it's an improvement overall. Out of all the times Nintendo has retold the Star Fox story, the Switch 2 version is easily the best-looking and best-acted. Star Fox 's campaign on Switch 2 is an improvement over the previous iterations of it thanks to these upgrades, but those that have already played the older versions extensively might find it hard to justify spending $59.99 on what is, at its core, an experience already available on Nintendo Switch 2 through the Switch Online Expansion Pack's N64 games library.

Star Fox Multiplayer is a Mixed Bag

Others will likely take issue with Star Fox 's co-op implementation. Instead of doing a proper co-op mode for the campaign, Star Fox on Switch 2 opts for one of Nintendo's cop-out co-op modes, which is simply a far inferior way to play the game. In Star Fox 's case, Nintendo basically splits functionality between the two players, so one person controls the Arwing's movements and the other controls the guns. It's not an ideal way to experience Star Fox , and it's hard to shake the feeling that the mode was tacked on for the sake of saying that the game has a co-op campaign. It's not true co-op, and if you go into this expecting true co-op, you are going to be greatly disappointed.

But when it comes to the biggest disappointment in the Star Fox remake on Switch 2, it's the complete lack of split-screen multiplayer. Star Fox 64 split-screen was a blast back in the day, but that's been removed with the Switch 2 version. Star Fox supports GameShare so it is at least a little easier to still have local multiplayer matches, but the lack of split-screen is the game's biggest misstep by far.

If you can get past the lack of split-screen, Star Fox's PvP multiplayer is fun, but light on content. Star Fox multiplayer battles are 4v4 fights where players can compete against local friends on other Switch consoles, bots, or online players. Star Fox only has three multiplayer maps at launch, and so it gets repetitive pretty quick, but multiplayer-specific power-ups and the competitive thrill keep it at least somewhat engaging.

"Beating" Star Fox on Nintendo Switch 2 takes 1.5 - 2 hours depending on skill level, but there are alternate routes, a Challenge Mode to tackle, and multiplayer to take into account.

While the multiplayer is fun, for the most part, if you have played Star Fox on Nintendo 64 or 3DS, you have effectively already played Star Fox on Nintendo Switch 2. That makes the $59.99 price tag hard to swallow, but if you are someone who has never played Star Fox before, you will likely get a lot more mileage out of the Switch 2 remake.

6 /10

Star Fox

  • Lackluster co-op mode
  • Multiplayer is light on content

Star Fox is available now, exclusively for the Nintendo Switch 2.

item.title: Riot Games Teases League of Legends Classic
item.description:
League of Legends developer Riot Games is officially teasing a "classic" game mode based on older versions of the long-running MOBA. The legacy version of League of Legends will likely be similar to classic servers offered by games such as World of Warcraft .

Throughout the more than 15 years since League of Legends launched, it has seen numerous changes. The classic Summoner's Rift map has gone through multiple overhauls during the game's different seasons, adding new jungle camps, changing up neutral objectives like the Dragon and Baron Nashor, and much more. The roster has also expanded significantly since launch, with more than 170 League of Legends Champions to choose from in modern version of the title. However, a new game mode being teased by Riot Games may be taking players back to some of the earliest days of the popular MOBA.

League of Legends May Be Adding a "Classic" Game Mode

Riot Games hinted at the upcoming reveal of League of Legends classic mode through an official teaser trailer. The comedic skit features League of Legends Executive Producer Paul "Pabro" Bellezza and League Studio head Andrei "Meddler" van Roon discussing what they consider the "good old days" of the MOBA. The video references numerous iconic elements of the game's rich history, ranging from old League of Legends items like Heart of Gold to the roster of 40 champions available during its beta. The video does not share many details about how League of Legends Classic will function in-game, with Riot planning to share more information during the finals of the MSI tournament on July 11.

Find all 10 pairs

Results

0
—
More Games
Find all 10 pairs
Regular Cover ArtClean Cover Art
Results
0
—
More Games

While Riot has not revealed many details about League of Legends Classic , the game mode has already sparked plenty of speculation. Many fans have theorized that the developer may bring the original version of League of Legends ' Runes system to League Classic , outfitting Champions with Runes that boost specific stats and a Mastery skill tree with more niche buffs. The time period represented in League of Legends Classic has been a point of contention, with the teaser suggesting that Riot Games may feature multiple different eras of League within the game mode. The video also suggested that players will once again be able to play the pre-reworked versions of classic Champions like Graves.

The classic mode will likely be an exciting new addition for longtime fans. Riot Games has frequently reworked older members of League of Legends ' playable cast, with many like Sion and Poppy drastically changing from their previous iterations. The trailer also hinted at the return of previously removed items to League of Legends Classic , potentially allowing players to use fan-favorite items like Deathfire Grasp and Frozen Mallet. The company did not confirm an official release date the potential classic mode in the teaser trailer.

Test your knowledge.

Sign in to claim your place on the leaderboard!

Leaderboard
No scores yet. Be the first.
1/10

Question text goes here?

Results

0 Points

—

Sign in to claim your place on the leaderboard!

Leaderboard
No scores yet. Be the first.

The classic version of the beloved MOBA comes as League of Legends has expanded more than ever in recent years. Riot Games has helped to develop numerous spin-offs, including 2XKO , Teamfight Tactics , and the in-development League of Legends MMORPG. The dev even launched a trading card game based on the MOBA in October 2025, with Riftbound debuting its Unleashed expansion in May and already confirming the Vendetta and Radiance sets to launch later this year. League of Legends fans have more ways to experience the game's universe than ever before and may soon have a chance to revisit its origins.

League of Legends

Follow
Followed
MOBA
Systems
OpenCritic Reviews
Top Critic Avg: 84 /100 Critics Rec: 90%
Released
October 27, 2009
ESRB
T for Teen: Blood, Fantasy Violence, Mild Suggestive Themes, Use of Alcohol and Tobacco
Developer(s)
Riot Games
Publisher(s)
Riot Games

WHERE TO PLAY

DIGITAL

Genre(s)
MOBA
Powered by
item.content:encoded:
League of Legends developer Riot Games is officially teasing a "classic" game mode based on older versions of the long-running MOBA. The legacy version of League of Legends will likely be similar to classic servers offered by games such as World of Warcraft .

Throughout the more than 15 years since League of Legends launched, it has seen numerous changes. The classic Summoner's Rift map has gone through multiple overhauls during the game's different seasons, adding new jungle camps, changing up neutral objectives like the Dragon and Baron Nashor, and much more. The roster has also expanded significantly since launch, with more than 170 League of Legends Champions to choose from in modern version of the title. However, a new game mode being teased by Riot Games may be taking players back to some of the earliest days of the popular MOBA.

League of Legends May Be Adding a "Classic" Game Mode

Riot Games hinted at the upcoming reveal of League of Legends classic mode through an official teaser trailer. The comedic skit features League of Legends Executive Producer Paul "Pabro" Bellezza and League Studio head Andrei "Meddler" van Roon discussing what they consider the "good old days" of the MOBA. The video references numerous iconic elements of the game's rich history, ranging from old League of Legends items like Heart of Gold to the roster of 40 champions available during its beta. The video does not share many details about how League of Legends Classic will function in-game, with Riot planning to share more information during the finals of the MSI tournament on July 11.

The video also suggested that players will once again be able to play the pre-reworked versions of classic Champions like Graves.

The classic mode will likely be an exciting new addition for longtime fans. Riot Games has frequently reworked older members of League of Legends ' playable cast, with many like Sion and Poppy drastically changing from their previous iterations. The trailer also hinted at the return of previously removed items to League of Legends Classic , potentially allowing players to use fan-favorite items like Deathfire Grasp and Frozen Mallet. The dev even launched a trading card game based on the MOBA in October 2025, with Riftbound debuting its Unleashed expansion in May and already confirming the Vendetta and Radiance sets to launch later this year. League of Legends fans have more ways to experience the game's universe than ever before and may soon have a chance to revisit its origins.
